title: AssetRegistryLink
description: Binds a Control to a concrete asset (system, vendor, data-store, API, model, dataset, region) in the tenant's asset registry. Materialises the abstract Control → real-world asset edge so audit packs can answer 'which systems are subject to this control?'.

asset_link_id.description: Stable identifier for this binding. Convention ALNK-<TOPIC>-NNN.

control_id.description: Control reference (e.g. CTRL-IAM-ACCESS-REVIEW-001) that applies to this asset.

asset_type.description: Kind of asset the binding points at.

asset_ref.description: Tenant-scoped identifier for the asset in the tenant's CMDB/registry. Opaque to the framework — the tenant defines the namespace.

tenant_id.description: Owner tenant. Cross-tenant references are forbidden by the Cerbos policy.

in_scope.description: Whether this asset is in scope for the control as of established_at. False = scope-out documented; the binding is preserved for audit history.

scope_rationale.description: Why the asset is in (or out of) scope. Required when in_scope=false.

established_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the binding was established.

expires_at.description: Optional end-of-life for the binding (e.g. vendor contract end, system decommission). After this the binding moves to historical.

owner_role.description: Role that owns the binding (e.g. ciso, dpo, head_of_infra). Matches the Cerbos role-name convention (snake_case).
